title: Teeing off and stepping up for charity
description: OUDTSHOORN NEWS - The Mayoral and Infantry...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"OUDTSHOORN NEWS - The Mayoral and Infantry School Golf Day, along with the inaugural Endurance Walk, brought sporting excitement to Oudtshoorn this past weekend. Both events drew strong participation and delivered positive results, capped off by a memorable Gala Dinner hosted at the Infantry School.","html":"OUDTSHOORN NEWS - The Mayoral and Infantry School Golf Day, along with the inaugural Endurance Walk, brought sporting excitement to Oudtshoorn this past weekend. Both events drew strong participation and delivered positive results, capped off by a memorable Gala Dinner hosted at the Infantry School."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The festive spirit continued into the evening, with the Infantry School Choir providing entertainment as guests enjoyed a gourmet meal."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"These celebrations reflected the weekend's emphasis not only on sport and camaraderie but also on giving back to the community.","html":"These celebrations reflected the weekend's emphasis not only on sport and camaraderie but also on giving back to the community."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Funds raised will support the Itemba Shelter for the Homeless in Oudtshoorn, Pediatric Care Africa and local cancer associations.","html":"Funds raised will support the Itemba Shelter for the Homeless in Oudtshoorn, Pediatric Care Africa and local cancer associations."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Colonel Tiisetso Sekgobela thanked all involved for their support. \"Your generosity, enthusiasm, and participation have made a significant difference. Together, we've raised funds for social responsibility programs that will bring hope and support to those in need. We're grateful for your kindness and look forward to continuing our mission together.\"","html":"Colonel Tiisetso Sekgobela thanked all involved for their support. \"Your generosity, enthusiasm, and participation have made a significant difference. Together, we've raised funds for social responsibility programs that will bring hope and support to those in need. We're grateful for your kindness and look forward to continuing our mission together.\""},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Organisers hailed the weekend as a success, seamlessly blending sport, community spirit, and charity.","html":"Organisers hailed the weekend as a success, seamlessly blending sport, community spirit, and charity."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Results","html":"Results"},{"type":"list","items":["In the golf day, 74 players competed, with the Mayoral/Infantry Team of Johan Allers, Col. Tiisetso Sekgobela, Jodi Mulder, and André Plaatjies securing first place with 68 points.","The KKNK Development Team, consisting of Peter Mooney, Phil Mooney, PG Mooney and Howard Ellis, took second place with 61 points.","The Total Energy Development Team with Merlin Masimela, Jason Maswanganyi, Eldrigde Booysen and Jean Heunis was in third place with 39 points.","Carson Solomons, Shirwyn Motha, Myron Pedro and Ruben Weber of the FNB Team played the most golf for the day with 38 points.","Nearest to the pin men: Gabriel Malgas","Longest drive men: André Plaatjies on 13."],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Teeing off and stepping up for charity
